recentTweets.text:
Some losses are hard to put into words. Larry Selders was a dear friend of mine and an amazing man, and Louisiana will not be the same without him. My thoughts, prayers, and condolences are with Kendra, LJ, Luke, and everyone who loved him. 

Rest well, my friend.

To read my https://t.co/NLIL902T9C

Heartbroken by the passing of Senator Larry Selders. He was a great man who gave everything to the people of Louisiana, and this loss will be felt deeply across our state. Praying for his wife, his sons, and all who loved him. Rest in peace, my friend.

Thank you, Natchitoches, for joining me at last week’s town hall. The change we want to see starts with us being present and engaging with the issues that affect our Louisiana communities. Keep on showing up and keep on speaking out. #LA06 https://t.co/KeTc5fjdmn

This Memorial Day, we honor our fallen heroes who made the ultimate sacrifice in service to our nation. 

We will forever remember their courage, honor their legacy, and keep their loved ones in our prayers. https://t.co/pLcWuLVtpk

Our support for Black athletes cannot begin and end with entertainment. I went to Congress to fight for them on and off the field — and that’s exactly what I did today alongside the CBC and NAACP.

For my full statement, visit https://t.co/yWH3uGSNNO. 

#LA06 #CleoFields #CBC https://t.co/tiHvjur6pd

This past weekend, I had the incredible opportunity to speak at the Rainbow PUSH Saturday Morning Forum about recent developments to the Voting Rights Act and how best to stay engaged with our nation’s civic process.

It’s important that everyone stays engaged in the civic https://t.co/0b7qFZqQ8h

Yesterday I had the pleasure of sitting down with my long time friend @donlemonofficial and @nolathaprogressive to discuss how people nationwide can stay engaged and stay involved in our country’s civic process. 

Now more than ever, it’s important that everyone knows their https://t.co/nGkwVt8b7E

The demographics of this state demand fair representation. The history of this state demands it. And the people of Louisiana — ALL of the people — deserve nothing less. 

#LA06 #VotingRights #CleoFields #LouisianaStrong https://t.co/ieB7S1XySz

Absentee ballots have been cast. Early voting was about to start. https://t.co/xH7kBDchTU

I’ve said it before and I’ll say it again: this issue is bigger than District 6. This is about black voters throughout the Southern part of our country having the tools to let their voices be heard. 

The federal government should be doing everything in its power to provide fair https://t.co/SlGMMLukju

RT @WBRZ: State and national leaders say they are not giving up the fight after the Supreme Court struck down Louisiana's congressional map…

Yesterday, I spoke with @stephenasmith on his Straight Shooter with Stephen A show to talk about the Supreme Court decision on Louisiana v. Callais and how this will impact District 6 and the nation. 

It’s important for voters to know that representation is on the line and this https://t.co/4A1kq0DLj1

These tragedies are a sobering reminder that gun violence does not discriminate — it follows us from our streets to our shopping centers to our nation's capital. Too many guns are in the wrong hands, and too many people in our communities are suffering without access to the

When it comes to education, the people doing the work every day deserve a seat at the table.

I am grateful to have sat down with President D’Shay Oaks (LAE), President Larry Carter (LFT), Karen Triche (LAP), Cheryl Serrett (LSBA), and Executive Director David Claxton (LASSA) to https://t.co/19JAuWVp13

I extend my deepest condolences to the families of the pilot and co-pilot who lost their lives last night in the collision between an Air Canada Express flight and a fire rescue vehicle on the runway at LaGuardia Airport. This is a heartbreaking tragedy, and my thoughts are with

Our TSA officers, Coast Guard service members, and FEMA responders show up every single day to keep Americans safe — the least we can do is make sure they get paid. That's why yesterday I signed a discharge petition to force a vote on funding for these critical DHS agencies.

The President just told Americans to accept higher gas prices "for a little while... until the war ends." A war he started with no exit strategy, no congressional authorization, and no plan.
 
Working families don't have a "little while”,  they're paying bills today. This is

This morning, the United States, in coordination with Israel, launched strikes against Iran — a significant military action with serious consequences for our national security.

While I understand the gravity of the threat, the process here cannot be ignored. The War Powers Act

Today's ruling by the Supreme Court confirms what many of us have maintained from the very beginning: the President of the United States cannot unilaterally impose taxes on the American people without congressional authorization. We said it then, and the highest court in the land

Today, I join the nation in mourning the passing of Dr. Norman C. Francis. For generations of Louisianans, and for people across this country, he represented what steady, faithful leadership in education truly looks like.
 
During his nearly five decades leading Xavier University https://t.co/KKdjJkuiv8

This morning, we lost a giant in the fight for civil rights, justice, and equality. More than that, we lost a man of deep faith and conviction whose life helped bend the arc of this nation toward justice.

Reverend Jesse Jackson stood with the forgotten, spoke for those pushed to https://t.co/NywkNKdcuP
https://t.co/pzwgHRkrrO

This morning, Border Czar Tom Homan announced the withdrawal of 700 ICE agents from Minnesota. 

This decision is a direct consequence of the Department of Homeland Security’s unconstitutional overreach against American citizens and affirms my vote against yesterday’s proposed

Yesterday morning, ICE agents in Minneapolis used excessive force against an American citizen, Mr. Alex Pretti, resulting in his death. Afterwards, Secretary Kristi Noem publicly
defended the actions of the agents, characterizing the shooting as justified.

I strongly disagree

And even today, the dream still matters. We’re grateful for the path Dr. King paved and the example he set for generations to come. ✨#MLKDay https://t.co/6SDzu90mZ6
RT @RepJeffries: Inflation is up, job creation is down and the Republican economy is a complete disaster.

Today, I voted without hesitation in support of extending Affordable Care Act subsidies.
 
As I’ve said before, the people of Louisiana should not have to choose between putting food on the table, keeping the lights on, and being able to go to the doctor.
 
When Americans are

I am deeply disturbed by reports that an American citizen was shot during an operation involving Immigration and Customs Enforcement in Minneapolis today. Any use of deadly force by federal law enforcement demands immediate, transparent, and independent review. 

Federal officers
I have serious concerns about President Trump’s decision to take military actions against Venezuela without informing congressional leaders, and there is absolutely no excuse for not doing so!
Don’t miss what’s happening in District 6! Scan the QR code. #LA06 https://t.co/qMTO2GVvYE
The American people deserve answers. Today’s House vote to release the Epstein files is a step toward the transparency this country has long been owed. https://t.co/uBINwf7Tar

Today, I lost a very dear friend in Jim Bernhard. Jim wasn’t just a businessman. He was a true friend and one of the most genuine, generous souls I’ve ever known. His leadership and kindness touched so many, and words cannot express how dearly he will be missed. Debra and I https://t.co/OJoAelo87Q

Baton Rouge, thank you for showing up and speaking truth. Thursday’s town hall was a powerful exchange of ideas, concerns, and community strength. I hear you and I’m carrying your voices with me as we push for solutions and continue to stand up for what’s right. https://t.co/8FnJRqL8zH

Lafayette, I hear you and I’m fighting everyday for real solutions. Your voices give strength to this fight, and together we can deliver the change our state and nation deserve. Thank you for showing up and speaking out. https://t.co/ysQGgiGb4O

Shreveport, thank you to everyone who joined last night’s town hall! It was an honor to hear your concerns, ideas, and perspectives. I know many of you are frustrated with this government shutdown, and I understand why. Your voices remind me of how important it is to keep https://t.co/0DpcpErHVc

We’re one month into this shutdown, and enough is enough. Next week, I’ll be in Baton Rouge, Lafayette, and Shreveport to let you know what’s going on and hear directly from you. #LA06 https://t.co/8dWXedHXkr

It was an honor to join the Baton Rouge Chapter of Jack and Jill of America, Inc. for their Kids Congress: The People’s House event! These bright young minds learned about how government works, what a bill is, and why laws exist. They then brought it all to life through their own https://t.co/Y0aFHB35cf
President Trump can eat Argentinian beef, but I’ll be sticking to the local farm-raised beef from the farmers in my congressional district. #IStandWithUSCattlemenAndWomen

Today, I visited Northside High School in Lafayette to honor a true trailblazer in education: Ms. Frances Davis Shaw. 

At 80 years old, Ms. Shaw came out of retirement to continue shaping young minds due to the critical teacher shortage. I was proud to present her with a https://t.co/rpUTfEXwOQ
https://t.co/6Hh4lu5Hpq
RT @RepTroyCarter: Today #SCOTUS will hear arguments in Louisiana v. Callais, a case that could topple what remains of the #VotingRightsAct…

I have returned to Washington ready to do my part to get our government back open. Too many families and federal workers are being affected by this shutdown. It’s time to come together and get it done. https://t.co/ytO10VlbZy

This morning, I stopped by Baton Rouge Magnet High to keep the conversation with our young people going. The next generation is watching, listening, and preparing to lead. Their voices truly do matter now more than ever. https://t.co/hnr4j7hWQC

Today’s students are tomorrow’s leaders. Today, I returned to my alma mater, McKinley Senior High, to remind them that their voices matter, especially in times like these. In today’s political climate, it’s critical that our young people understand how government works and how https://t.co/Eq2YvaeGrM

If Republicans’ partisan bill stands, health care tax credits that help thousands in our district are on the line. I will keep fighting until the government is open again and your health care is protected. https://t.co/GOJvk5ejOE
A government shutdown threatens pay checks, services, and stability for millions. That’s why I’m speaking out. Stay tuned. I’m going to keep you informed. https://t.co/LMNfYMi7Tg

The threats that were made against HBCUs across this nation today, including Southern University here at home, were unacceptable and quite frankly, shameful. No student should ever have to fear for their safety while at school, and no parent should send their child off to college

The tragic death of Charlie Kirk is a reminder of the painful moment our country is living through. No matter our political differences, violence is unacceptable and has no place at all in our democracy. My thoughts and prayers are with Charlie Kirk’s family, especially his wife
